---
title: "Urban Taxonomy"
author: "Martin Fleischmann"
institute: "Charles University"
format:
    revealjs:
        theme: [default, ../assets/reveal.scss]
        logo: ../assets/icon.svg
        menu: false
        transition: fade
        navigation-mode: linear
        controls-layout: edges
        progress: true
        include-in-header:
            - ../assets/font.html
            - text: |
                <style>
                #title-slide .title {
                    font-size: 50px;
                }
                </style>
        title-slide-attributes:
            data-background-image: ../assets/full_logo.svg
            data-background-size: 20%
            data-background-position: 50% 90%
        max-scale: 10
---


# We have built something

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/taxonomy.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/taxonomy_tree.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/taxonomy_chars.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/taxonomy_abundance.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/taxonomy_mtopes.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/taxonomy.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

---


![](../figures/202511_ILUS/tree_nocut.png){fig-align="center" width=60%}

::: aside
Taxonomic tree (up to 256 branches)
:::

---

![](../figures/202511_ILUS/tree_cut0.png){fig-align="center" width=60%}

::: aside
Taxonomic tree with a cut marking 2 branches (level 1)
:::


## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/examples_1.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

::: aside
Illustration of different levels of the hierarchy.
:::

---

![](../figures/202511_ILUS/tree_cut1.png){fig-align="center" width=60%}

::: aside
Taxonomic tree with a cut marking 4 branches (level 2)
:::

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/examples_2.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

::: aside
Illustration of different levels of the hierarchy.
:::

---


![](../figures/202511_ILUS/tree_cut2.png){fig-align="center" width=60%}

::: aside
Taxonomic tree with a cut marking 8 branches (level 3)
:::

## {background-image="../figures/202506_ISUF/examples.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

::: aside
Illustration of different levels of the hierarchy.
:::

# Urban Taxonomy

## {background-iframe="https://uscuni.org/himoc/" background-size="contain" .no-text}

::: aside
[urbantaxonomy.org](https://urbantaxonomy.org)
:::

# What now? {.question}

# Geography and urban structure

---

![](../figures/202603_BEGIN/abundance_l1.png){fig-align="center" width=50%}

::: aside
Abundance of built fabric types derived from level 1
:::

---

![](../figures/202603_BEGIN/abundance_l2.png){fig-align="center"}

::: aside
Abundance of built fabric types derived from level 2
:::

---

![](../figures/202603_BEGIN/abundance_l3.png){fig-align="center"}

::: aside
Abundance of built fabric types derived from level 3
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/europe.png" background-size="fill" .no-text}

---

![](../figures/202511_ILUS/tree_cut3.png){fig-align="center" width=60%}

::: aside
Taxonomic tree with a cut marking 107 branches (level 7)
:::

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/branch_proportions.png" background-size="contain" .no-text}

---

::: {.r-fit-text .absolute top=39%}
Compression to 2 dimensions using UMAP
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_components.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
UMAP components reflecting the composition within 50km grid.
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_eastern_germany.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_eastern-poland.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_pl_outliers.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_cz-pl.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_galicia.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_be.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_be_top.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_be_mid.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

---

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/umap_be_nl.png" background-size="contain" .no-text data-transition="none"}

::: aside
Projection of UMAP components
:::

## {background-image="../figures/202511_ILUS/belgium.png" background-size="fill" .no-text}


# Cities in time

---

![Amsterdam](../figures/202607_Nanjing/amsterdam.png){fig-align="center"}



---

![Madrid](../figures/202607_Nanjing/madrid.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![Paris](../figures/202607_Nanjing/paris.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![Sevilla](../figures/202607_Nanjing/sevilla.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![Zaragoza](../figures/202607_Nanjing/zaragoza.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![Spain, level 1](../figures/202607_Nanjing/es1.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![Spain, level 2](../figures/202607_Nanjing/es2.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![Spain, level 3](../figures/202607_Nanjing/es3.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![Spain, level 4](../figures/202607_Nanjing/es4.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![France, level 1](../figures/202607_Nanjing/fr1.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![France, level 2](../figures/202607_Nanjing/fr2.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![France, level 3](../figures/202607_Nanjing/fr3.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![France, level 4](../figures/202607_Nanjing/fr4.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![building alignment](../figures/202607_Nanjing/building_alignment.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![mean distance to neighbouring buildings](../figures/202607_Nanjing/neighbor_distance.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![joined perimeter wall length](../figures/202607_Nanjing/perimeter_wall.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![street width](../figures/202607_Nanjing/street_width.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![building squareness [FR]](../figures/202607_Nanjing/squareness.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![building squareness [ES]](../figures/202607_Nanjing/squareness_es.png){fig-align="center"}


# Environmental effects

---

![](../figures/202607_Hangzhou/tmin.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![](../figures/202607_Hangzhou/tmin_delta_l1.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![](../figures/202607_Hangzhou/tmin_delta_l2.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![](../figures/202607_Hangzhou/tmin_delta_l3.png){fig-align="center"}

---

![](../figures/202607_Nanjing/eta2.png){fig-align="center"}

# Population effects

---

## Scale {background-image="../figures/202602_urrlab_Anna/detail.png".no-text}

::: aside
Basic Settlement Units 

20 000 BSU in Czechia

Delineated based on urban fabric
:::

---

::: {.r-fit-text .absolute top=39%}
Census characterstics as the independent variables
:::

---

## Census variables

::: {.column width="50%"}


Population density

Age structure

Employment sector

Employment status

Dwelling ownership


:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

Education level

Household size

Citizenship

Religion

Marital status


:::


---


## Census processing


![](../figures/202602_urrlab_Anna/diagram.png){fig-align="center"}


## Results {data-transition="none"}

![](../figures/202602_urrlab_Anna/acc.png){fig-align="center"}

::: aside
Averaged F1-macro score.
:::

---


## Results {data-transition="none"}

![](../figures/202602_urrlab_Anna/acc_linear.png){fig-align="center"}

::: aside
Averaged F1-macro score.
:::

---


## Results {data-transition="none"}

![](../figures/202602_urrlab_Anna/acc_strong.png){fig-align="center"}

::: aside
Averaged F1-macro score.
:::

---

![](../figures/202607_Nanjing/boxplot.png){fig-align="center"}

::: aside
Distribution of local coefficients/importances ordered by their magnitude
:::

---


![](../figures/202607_Nanjing/boxplot-form.png){fig-align="center"}

::: aside
Distribution of standard deviations of local coefficients/importances by fabric type
:::

---

![](../figures/202607_Nanjing/side_by_side.png){fig-align="center"}

::: aside

:::


# Take home points

---

::: {.r-fit-text .absolute top=39%}
Urban morphometrics can support<br>quantitative studies done at scale.
:::

---

::: {.r-fit-text .absolute top=39%}
Structure of cities is deeply affected<br>by culture and politics.
:::

---

::: {.r-fit-text .absolute top=39%}
We can read the story of urban development.
:::

---

::: {.r-fit-text .absolute top=39%}
And learn how it affects the present.
:::


---

## Do you want to follow up {.question}

[{{< fa solid person-chalkboard >}}](https://uscuni.org/talks/slides/202607_Nanjing.html) uscuni.org/talks

[{{< fa solid map >}}](https://urbantaxonomy.org) urbantaxonomy.org

[{{< fa solid code >}}](https://github.com/uscuni/urban_taxonomy) github.com/uscuni/urban_taxonomy

[{{< fa regular envelope >}}](mailto:martin.fleischmann@natur.cuni.cz) martin.fleischmann@natur.cuni.cz

[{{< fa regular user >}}](https://martinfleischmann.net) martinfleischmann.net

::: aside
The authors kindly acknowledge funding by the Charles University’s Primus programme through the project "Influence of Socioeconomic and Cultural Factors on Urban Structure in Central Europe", project reference `PRIMUS/24/SCI/023`.
:::
